content.problemStatement:
Utkarsh has a permutation PPP of {0,1,2,вЂ¦,Nв€’1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,вЂ¦,Nв€’1}.

He wants to find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1в‰¤L<Rв‰¤N)(1 \leq L \lt R \leq N)(1в‰¤L<Rв‰¤N) such that swapping the elements PLP_LPLвЂ‹ and PRP_RPRвЂ‹ does not change the MEX of any subarray.

Formally, find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1в‰¤L<Rв‰¤N)(1\leq L \lt R \leq N)(1в‰¤L<Rв‰¤N) satisfying the following condition:


Let PвЂІP'PвЂІ be the permutation defined as follows:

PiвЂІ=PiP'_i = P_iPiвЂІвЂ‹=PiвЂ‹ for iв€€{1,2,3,вЂ¦,N}в€–{L,R}i \in \{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N\} \setminus \{L, R\}iв€€{1,2,3,вЂ¦,N}в€–{L,R}
PLвЂІ=PRP'_L = P_RPLвЂІвЂ‹=PRвЂ‹
PRвЂІ=PLP'_R = P_LPRвЂІвЂ‹=PLвЂ‹


mex({Pi,Pi+1,вЂ¦,Pj})=mex({PiвЂІ,Pi+1вЂІ,вЂ¦,PjвЂІ})mex(\{P_i, P_{i+1}, \ldots, P_j\}) = mex(\{P'_i, P'_{i+1}, \ldots, P'_j\})mex({PiвЂ‹,Pi+1вЂ‹,вЂ¦,PjвЂ‹})=mex({PiвЂІвЂ‹,Pi+1вЂІвЂ‹,вЂ¦,PjвЂІвЂ‹}) for every 1в‰¤iв‰¤jв‰¤N1 \leq i \leq j \leq N1в‰¤iв‰¤jв‰¤N
For example, let N=7N = 7N=7 and P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]P = [2, 5, 1, 0, 3, 6, 4]P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]. Then,


Swapping the pair (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), i.e swapping P1=2P_1 = 2P1вЂ‹=2 and P3=1P_3 = 1P3вЂ‹=1 would give us the permutation PвЂІ=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]P' = [1, 5, 2, 0, 3, 6, 4]PвЂІ=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]. This changes the mex of the subarray [3,4][3, 4][3,4], because mex({P3,P4})=mex({1,0})=2mex(\{P_3, P_4\}) = mex(\{1, 0\}) = 2mex({P3вЂ‹,P4вЂ‹})=mex({1,0})=2 but mex({P3вЂІ,P4вЂІ})=mex({2,0})=1mex(\{P'_3, P'_4\}) = mex(\{2, 0\}) = 1mex({P3вЂІвЂ‹,P4вЂІвЂ‹})=mex({2,0})=1, so (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) is not a valid swap.
Swapping the pair (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) gives us the permutation PвЂІ=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]P' = [2, 5, 1, 4, 3, 6, 0]PвЂІ=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]. This changes the mex of the subarray [7,7][7, 7][7,7] вЂ” mex({P7})=mex({4})=0mex(\{P_7\}) = mex(\{4\}) = 0mex({P7вЂ‹})=mex({4})=0 and mex({P7вЂІ})=mex({0})=1mex(\{P'_7\}) = mex(\{0\}) = 1mex({P7вЂІвЂ‹})=mex({0})=1. So, (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) is also not a valid pair.
However, it can be verified that the pair (2,6)(2, 6)(2,6) is valid вЂ” swapping P2P_2P2вЂ‹ and P6P_6P6вЂ‹ gives us the permutation PвЂІ=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4]P' = [2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4]PвЂІ=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4], and mex(P[iвЂ¦j])=mex(PвЂІ[iвЂ¦j])mex(P[i \ldots j]) = mex(P'[i \ldots j])mex(P[iвЂ¦j])=mex(PвЂІ[iвЂ¦j]) for every 1в‰¤iв‰¤jв‰¤71 \leq i \leq j \leq 71в‰¤iв‰¤jв‰¤7.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Description of each test case:
The first line contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,вЂ¦,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1вЂ‹,P2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,PNвЂ‹, denoting the permutation PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs (L,R)(L,R)(L,R) such that swapping PLP_LPLвЂ‹ and PRP_RPRвЂ‹ does not change the MEX of any subarray.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2в‹…1052 \cdot 10^52в‹…105
It is guaranteed that PPP is a permutation of {0,1,2,вЂ¦,Nв€’1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,вЂ¦,Nв€’1}.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are no valid pairs.
Test case 222: There is only one valid pair, (L,R)=(2,6)(L,R) = (2,6)(L,R)=(2,6), i.e, swapping P2=5P_2 = 5P2вЂ‹=5 and P6=6P_6 = 6P6вЂ‹=6 to obtain the permutation [2,6,1,0,3,5,4][2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4][2,6,1,0,3,5,4].
